patch.value:
## Why (2026-07-11 maintainer directive: too many too-long files by effective LOC; document-only pass)
Effective-LOC census (non-blank, non-comment; gate counts the same way since PR#506, cap 3400):
main.ts 3369 (99%) · search.ts 3330 (jt3b) · extensions/loader.ts 3312 · commands/extension.ts 3239 · sdk/runtime.ts 3236 · validate.ts 3052 (oxrw) · register-mutation.ts 3032 · create.ts 2851 · completion.ts 2809 · update.ts 2758 · extension/scaffold.ts 2739 · test.ts 2713 (oslr) · health.ts 2460 (oxrw) · telemetry/runtime.ts 2366 · contracts.ts 2358 · plan.ts 2095 (je50).
The old LOC growth watch (pm-k1im) and main.ts items (pm-6c3h, pm-sh6o) are closed; nothing OPEN owns the infrastructure files, and main.ts is one feature away from failing the gate.

## Ownership fences (never double-file)
- search.ts → pm-jt3b (query-parser + inline-field split already scoped).
- Command files in the promotion partition get FIRST relief from their SDK slice (validate/health → pm-oxrw, schema/config/profile/init → pm-3mna, plan → pm-je50, test/context → pm-oslr, annotations → pm-zwpp, history-maintenance → pm-4a7m); decompose only the presentation remainder if still near cap afterwards.
- THIS item owns the files outside that partition: main.ts, core/extensions/loader.ts, sdk/runtime.ts, cli/register-mutation.ts, cli/commands/completion.ts (+ commands/extension.ts + scaffold.ts if their slice relief is insufficient).

## Split seams (follow the PR#396 split playbook: extract at same call position, public surface byte-identical)
- main.ts → continue the pm-6c3h/pm-sh6o per-command-registration extraction; bootstrap/help/global-flag blocks are self-contained.
- loader.ts → manifest-parse / activation / sandbox-boundary seams (version-compat already extracted, pm-sjea precedent).
- sdk/runtime.ts → per-domain action tables + shared dispatch kernel (keeps runAction semantics-oracle role intact).
- register-mutation.ts → per-mutation registration modules mirroring commands/index.ts layout.
- completion.ts → data tables (flag strings per shell) out of logic; tables are generated-ish and cheap to isolate.
LOC-cap headroom: decompose near-cap infrastructure files outside the SDK-promotion partition (main.ts at 99% of the 3400 gate)

main.ts, extensions/loader.ts, sdk/runtime.ts, register-mutation.ts, completion.ts each at or under 80% of the 3400 effective-line gate; splits are behavior-preserving (byte-identical outputs on goldens, contract tests green); vitest.config mirrored test paths updated; jscpd clean; coverage gate holds 100%; no decomposition of files fenced to other owners

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
2026-07-12 census refresh (same method: static-quality-gate normalizeLine over src/**/*.ts, cap 3400). Delta vs the 07-11 body census:
- sdk/runtime.ts 3236 -> 3388 (99.6% of cap, 12 effective lines of headroom) — now the #1 at-risk file, overtaking main.ts (3369, unchanged). Growth is the SDK-first slices themselves: PR#512 (plan), PR#517 (ranked atomic selection), PR#520 (schema boundaries) all land action surface here.
- SEQUENCING CONSEQUENCE: pm-3mna S1 (next SDK slice, current recommended frontier) cannot merge without blowing the gate. The runtime.ts seam owned here (per-domain action tables + shared dispatch kernel, runAction semantics-oracle preserved) must land FIRST — either as a standalone prep PR or as 3mna S1's first commit. Coordination note posted on pm-usfg.
- context.ts crossed 2000 (2011) — watch, not owned here (context belongs to pm-oslr slice relief first).
- create.ts 2851->2789, update.ts 2758->2697 (PR#523 relief); register-mutation 3037, completion 2818 (+5/+9 noise); search.ts/loader.ts/extension.ts/validate.ts unchanged.
- Files >=3000: 7; >=2000: 17. Priority bumped 2->1 on the strength of the runtime.ts headroom.

author: maintainer-agent
message: sdk/runtime.ts at 3388/3400 after PR#512/#517/#520 — runtime seam now gates SDK-partition entry (see 2026-07-12 census note)

author: codex-root
message: Start SDK runtime decomposition required for wide primitive promotion

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation start: live frontier confirms sdk/runtime.ts has 12 effective lines of headroom; this active item owns a behavior-preserving per-domain dispatch split bundled with pm-3mna and pm-zwpp.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Execution rule from user escalation (23rd recurrence): this active delivery lane must not stop at partial implementation, local verification, commit, PR creation, pending checks, or pending bot review. Continue through the requested green reviewed PR, merge, merged-head/release/Sentry/telemetry proof, tracker closure, claim release, and return to main; only a genuine exhausted blocker permits stopping.

value.note: removed annotation command private-core boundary entries

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Evidence: annotation extraction removed one CLI-private domain module and 13 private-core baseline imports while keeping the static boundary gate green. The broader sdk/runtime.ts per-domain dispatch decomposition remains open and is not claimed complete.

author: codex-root
message: Release after annotation-boundary relief; runtime dispatch decomposition remains

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Census delta 2026-07-12: src/cli/main.ts 3369->3376/3400 (+7 effective, canonical normalizeLine method) — reset-on-entry seam for runtime-extension snapshot caches (pm-8fxc fix inside runPmCli). sdk/runtime.ts steady 3388/3400 after PR#524 (annotation kernel landed as NEW src/sdk/annotations.ts — precedent that new SDK domains can bypass the runtime.ts cap; see pm-usfg frontier note). Decomposition seam priorities unchanged.
